$description: Single source of truth for all ASE-OS website content. Synced to ASE-OS-Website via `aiw sync --website`.

meta.title: ASE-OS — AI Software Engineering Operating System
meta.description: A unified, skill-driven, event-driven AI engineering system that designs, generates, tests, and documents software autonomously.

openGraph.title: ASE-OS — AI Software Engineering Operating System
openGraph.description: A unified, skill-driven AI system that takes your idea from requirements to deployed software — automatically, with zero documentation drift.

twitter.title: ASE-OS — AI Software Engineering Operating System
twitter.description: Skill-driven AI engineering. Full requirements traceability. Ideas to deployed software, automatically.

headline: AI Software
Engineering OS
hero.subheadline: A unified, skill-driven system that takes your idea all the way from requirements to deployed software — automatically, with no manual wiring and no documentation drift.

features.sectionSubtitle: Every capability is built-in, automatic, and non-bypassable — not a plugin or an option.

items.title: Architecture Enforcement
items.description: Every code change is checked against the architecture before it runs. If code crosses a module boundary it shouldn't, the system blocks it — not just warns.

items.title: Automatic Follow-Through
items.description: Change a file and the system automatically kicks off code review, test checks, doc updates, and impact analysis. No manual steps, no forgetting.

items.title: Security That Can't Be Skipped
items.description: When a change touches authentication, sessions, or data access, a security review runs automatically. There is no override path.

items.title: Code From Specs, Not Prompts
items.description: All generated code comes from architecture specs. Every file is traceable to a module definition and carries a @req annotation — nothing is invented on the fly.

items.title: Tests That Stay Current
items.description: Tests are generated from the code that was written. When code changes, new tests are generated to match — automatically.

items.title: Docs That Never Fall Behind
items.description: Documentation is generated from the live system state. Every code or architecture change triggers an automatic sync — docs are always current.

items.title: One-Step Rollback
items.description: The system takes a snapshot before every change. If something breaks, rolling back to the last known-good state takes a single step.

items.title: Token Budget Guardrails
items.description: Every operation has a hard token ceiling. When context grows too large, the context-compressor fires automatically — cycling through three levels (light → medium → aggressive) until the payload fits. Nothing silently goes over budget, and you see an efficiency score for every compression.

items.title: Four-Layer Guard System
items.description: Before any release, four guards run in parallel: database safety, N+1 query detection, UI/UX compliance, and release readiness. A single block halts the pipeline.

items.title: Release Readiness Score
items.description: Before deployment, the system cross-checks every requirement against code, tests, UI screens, DB entities, and docs. You get a 0–100 readiness score and full traceability matrix.

items.title: End-to-End Traceability
items.description: Every task carries acceptance criteria and Definition of Done items. Code is annotated with @req tags. You can trace any requirement to the exact file and test that implements it.

items.title: Intelligent Prompt Routing
items.description: Vague prompts are normalized before they reach the pipeline. The prompt-normalizer extracts intent and either routes confidently or asks a single targeted clarification — never guesses.

items.title: Session Observability
items.description: After every pipeline stage, telemetry is captured — skill latency, HITL outcomes, token usage — and analyzed into a read-only enhancement dashboard. Zero PII. Feeds the adaptation engine.

items.title: Assisted Self-Enhancement
items.description: The system analyzes its own usage patterns and proposes ranked improvements — new skills, modified pipelines, or retired dead weight. Every proposal requires your approval before anything changes.

items.title: Structured Work Items
items.description: Every task, bug, review, and change request is a typed work item with a lifecycle state machine. Defect chains link root cause to fix to closure. Export to Jira-compatible format with one command.

items.title: Domain Specialist Agents
items.description: Four specialist agents — AI/LLM systems, Mobile (iOS/Android/RN/Flutter), SaaS & Enterprise (multi-tenant, SOC 2), and Embedded/IoT — auto-route into the pipeline when your project requires them.

items.title: Motion & Creative Design
items.description: Three creative-layer skills cover motion design, UX research synthesis, and creative experience architecture. They run in Phase 2 alongside the core UX architect so visual and functional design stay in sync.

items.title: Gap-to-Skill Pipeline
items.description: When the orchestrator detects a capability the system can't handle, it fires a gap event. A reactive pipeline drafts a new skill, scores it for quality, and presents it for your approval — before anything is registered.

items.title: Behavioral Change Detection
items.description: Goes beyond line-level diffs to detect logic shifts, contract changes, and security boundary alterations. Feeds precise impact signals to security review and test generation — so tests are rewritten when the contract changes, not just when lines change.

items.title: Built-In Compliance Mapping
items.description: Maps every feature and architecture decision against GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 clauses at design time — before a line of code is written. Produces a traceability matrix, gap report, and audit evidence checklist automatically.

items.title: Cloud Cost Intelligence
items.description: Projects monthly AWS, GCP, and Azure costs directly from the deployment strategy output — before you commit to an architecture. Produces per-provider breakdowns, three alternative configurations, and cost-per-feature attribution.

items.title: Pipeline Dry-Run
items.description: Runs the full pipeline in simulation mode before any real execution. Every skill fires, outputs are generated in preview form, no disk writes occur. You get a unified report: files that would change, HITL gates that would trigger, block risks, and a go/no-go summary.

items.title: API Lifecycle Management
items.description: Manages the full API version lifecycle from current to deprecated to sunset. Auto-generates migration guides from breaking changes, creates consumer migration work items, and enforces sunset timelines with automatic violation detection.

items.title: A/B Architecture Comparison
items.description: Forks the pipeline at architecture-design into two named branches, runs each through planning and quality skills in parallel, and produces a side-by-side scorecard with a recommendation. A mandatory HITL gate selects the winner before any artifacts are promoted.

items.title: Data Platform Intelligence
items.description: Five data-engineering skills cover the full pipeline lifecycle: batch ETL/streaming design, dbt data quality rules, ML feature store and serving architecture, dimensional analytics schemas, and data contract enforcement between producers and consumers.

items.title: API Contract First
items.description: Three API design skills produce binding contracts before code is written: OpenAPI 3.1 REST specs with versioning and rate-limiting plans, GraphQL schemas with Apollo Federation topology and authorization directives, and AsyncAPI event catalogs with schema registry enforcement.

items.title: Microservices & DDD
items.description: Six distributed-systems skills cover DDD bounded context mapping, microservice decomposition with Conway's Law analysis, CQRS/event-sourcing aggregate design, resilience patterns (circuit breakers, sagas, FMEA), multi-tier caching topologies, and real-time WebSocket/CRDT architectures.

items.title: Cloud-Native Architecture
items.description: Three cloud-platform skills deliver: Well-Architected Framework reviews across AWS, GCP, and Azure with a prioritized remediation roadmap; serverless function topology design with cold-start mitigation; and Kubernetes cluster design with Helm charts, GitOps pipelines, and RBAC models.

items.title: Security Depth Layer
items.description: Three security-specialist skills go deeper than the base security review: STRIDE threat modeling with MITRE ATT&CK mappings, HashiCorp Vault architecture with secret rotation and PKI design, and DevSecOps pipeline stages with SAST/DAST/SCA/SBOM and SLSA compliance gates.

items.title: SRE & Reliability Engineering
items.description: Five SRE skills operationalize reliability: SLI/SLO/error budget design, k6/Gatling load test scenarios with SLO-gated thresholds, CPU flamegraph and query plan profiling, alert-linked runbook generation with escalation trees, and chaos engineering experiments with GameDay plans and CI chaos gates.

items.title: MCP Tool Integration
items.description: Six live MCP servers give every agent access to GitHub, real-time web search, persistent memory, URL fetching, up-to-date library docs (Context7), and headless browser automation — all without bash permissions.

principles.sectionSubtitle: Every decision in ASE-OS traces back to one of these three foundations.

items.title: Skill-Driven Execution
items.description: Every action the system takes runs through a named, versioned Skill — not a freeform prompt. Skills have fixed inputs, fixed outputs, and defined rules. If a capability doesn't have a Skill, it doesn't exist in the system.

items.title: Event-Driven Architecture
items.description: When something changes — code, architecture, tests, or docs — the system automatically emits an event and triggers all the right follow-up actions. You never have to manually kick off downstream work.

items.title: State-Centric Control
items.description: Everything the system knows lives in one structured state file. Agents can read it, but only the state-manager can write to it. Every change is tracked, versioned, and fully reversible.

orchestration.sectionSubtitle: The orchestrator decomposes the pipeline into phases, resolves the correct skill and agent for each phase, validates inputs, and dispatches agents in the right order — automatically.

decompositionSteps.label: Phase identified
decompositionSteps.desc: Orchestrator maps the prompt to a pipeline phase

decompositionSteps.label: Skill resolved
decompositionSteps.desc: Registry lookup returns the correct SKILL.md

decompositionSteps.label: Agent assigned
decompositionSteps.desc: Agent with matching skill binding is selected

decompositionSteps.label: Inputs validated
decompositionSteps.desc: Schema check: required inputs present and typed

decompositionSteps.label: Agent dispatched
decompositionSteps.desc: Agent invoked with scoped context + skill spec

aiExecution.sectionSubtitle: Each phase produces structured artifacts that become the inputs for the next. Complex reasoning agents use the standard model tier; high-volume utility tasks use a lightweight model to minimise cost. Every assignment is a single-line config change.

phases.output: Structured requirement objects with acceptance criteria
Module boundaries, data flow contracts, ADR records
Task graph with effort estimates and milestones
Unit tests, integration tests, edge-case suites
Updated docs — async, never blocks pipeline

backgroundProcessing.sectionSubtitle: Before any agent acts, the orchestrator assembles a targeted context window — pulling only the relevant slice of project knowledge using a four-layer memory architecture.

contextLayers.desc: Active session messages and current pipeline state. Lives in the LLM context, cleared between sessions.
Persistent pipeline execution state stored in session_state.json. Survives session boundaries. Covers pipeline progress, ADRs, and skill outputs.
AST-level code graph spanning your entire codebase. Cross-file relationships, community clusters, and semantic navigation — no vector DB required.
PII-free ring buffer capturing skill latency, HITL outcomes, and token usage per session. Capped at 500 events. Feeds session-insights analysis at pipeline completion.

discoverySteps.label: Check session state
discoverySteps.desc: Load session_state.json if a prior session exists for this feature

discoverySteps.label: Query the graph
discoverySteps.desc: Run `graphify query "<feature>"` — returns a scoped subgraph, not the entire codebase

discoverySteps.label: Load skill specs
discoverySteps.desc: Mount required SKILL.md files for this pipeline into context

discoverySteps.label: Evaluate pressure
discoverySteps.desc: If token budget > 80%, trigger context-compressor before proceeding

qualityAssurance.sectionSubtitle: Four overlapping QA layers run before any artifact is considered done. The release guard is the final non-bypassable threshold check — there is no override path.

stages.title: Code Review
stages.desc: Checks SOLID principles, anti-patterns, complexity thresholds, and duplication. Produces a scored report.

stages.title: Testing Strategy & Test Run
stages.desc: Defines test plan and generates full unit/integration/edge-case suites. Coverage must hit the quality gate threshold.

stages.title: Security Review
stages.desc: Mandatory and non-skippable when any security boundary is touched. Blocks the pipeline until all findings are resolved.

stages.title: Guard Skills
stages.desc: Five guards run in parallel before the pipeline leaves the quality stage: database safety, N+1 query detection, UI/UX compliance, CVSS security threshold, and work item lifecycle validation. Any single block verdict halts the pipeline.

outputDelivery.sectionSubtitle: When all gates pass, the pipeline produces five categories of deliverable — and a full human approval trail shows every decision point that was reviewed before deployment.

deliverables.title: Production-Ready Code
deliverables.desc: Reviewed, tested, and security-cleared implementation files. All specs enforced — nothing invented beyond what was specified.

deliverables.title: Living Documentation
deliverables.desc: Auto-generated and synced docs — ADRs, API reference, README, onboarding guide. No documentation drift. Runs asynchronously, never blocks delivery.

deliverables.title: Deployment Artifacts
deliverables.desc: Deployment strategy, environment promotion rules, feature flags, and IaC scaffold — all generated from the same pipeline run.

deliverables.title: Rollback Plan
deliverables.desc: A rollback snapshot is committed to session state before every deployment. One command reverts to the prior known-good state if anything goes wrong.

deliverables.title: Work Item Export
deliverables.desc: The complete task graph exported as Jira-compatible work items — tasks, bugs, reviews, change requests, and closures — ready for import into any external project management tool. Runs async, never blocks delivery.

promptSubmission.sectionSubtitle: You describe what you want in plain language. The orchestrator normalizes your input, detects intent, and dispatches the right pipeline — before any AI agent does any work.

routingExamples.prompt: Analyze requirements for a payment gateway
Design the architecture for a real-time notification system
Build a user authentication system with JWT and RBAC
Review this code for SOLID violations

normalizerSteps.label: Prompt received
normalizerSteps.detail: Raw natural language input from user

normalizerSteps.label: Intent routing
normalizerSteps.detail: Matched against routing table → pipeline selected

normalizerSteps.label: Pipeline dispatched
normalizerSteps.detail: Entry agent initialized with correct skill binding

promptSubmission.ambiguityWarning: If intent is ambiguous, the pipeline halts here and asks the user to clarify before proceeding.

phaseDescriptions.phase-1-requirements: The requirement-analyzer reads your raw input and turns it into structured requirement objects — each with acceptance criteria and flagged open questions. A human approval gate follows before any design work begins.
phaseDescriptions.phase-2-architecture: The architect designs module boundaries, data flow, and integration contracts. The ADR generator runs in the background to capture every major technical decision as a permanent, immutable record.
phaseDescriptions.phase-2b-design: The frontend-ux-architect designs user experience flows and component hierarchy. When a design system is required, design-system-generator creates the token files, component stubs, and Storybook configuration from the UX output.
phaseDescriptions.phase-2c-design-system: The design-system-generator produces the complete design system scaffold from the UX architecture output — token files, component stubs, Storybook configuration, and theme setup. This phase only runs when the project requires a component library or design system.
phaseDescriptions.phase-3-graph: The dependency-analyzer builds the full module dependency graph. It detects cycles (cross-module cycles fail the build; within-module cycles generate warnings) and calculates exactly which parts of the system are affected by any future change.
phaseDescriptions.phase-4-planning: Feature planning breaks the architecture into a task graph with dependencies, effort estimates, and milestones. The human approves the full roadmap before a single line of code is written.
phaseDescriptions.phase-5-impact: Before any code changes, the system calculates the full blast radius: which modules are affected, which API contracts break, which tests are invalidated, where documentation will drift, and whether any security boundaries are crossed.
phaseDescriptions.phase-6-execution: The code-generator turns architecture modules and feature plans into working implementation files, interface stubs, and scaffolding. It only generates what was specified — nothing is invented.
phaseDescriptions.phase-7-quality: Code review, testing strategy, security review, and test generation all run in parallel. Security review is mandatory and non-skippable when any security boundary is touched.
phaseDescriptions.phase-7b-guards: Guard skills run in parallel as a final checklist before the pipeline advances past the quality stage. Five guards run simultaneously: database-guard (destructive migrations, PII), performance-guard (N+1 queries, missing indexes), ui-ux-compliance-guard (design token violations, accessibility), security-guard (CVSS threshold, OWASP), and work-item-lifecycle-guard (invalid work item state transitions). A single block verdict from any one guard halts the pipeline.
phaseDescriptions.phase-8-repair: If tests fail or the build breaks, code-repair diagnoses the root cause and applies the minimal fix needed. After a maximum number of attempts, it escalates to the rollback manager. This phase is skipped entirely when everything passes.
phaseDescriptions.phase-8b-audit: The implementation-completeness-auditor cross-checks every requirement against delivered code, tests, UI screens, database entities, and documentation. It produces a readiness score (0–100) and a full traceability matrix. Any gap is classified as missing, stub, untested, or undocumented — and must be resolved before the release gate passes.
phaseDescriptions.phase-8c-release-guard: The release guard is the final non-bypassable threshold check. It verifies that quality scores, test coverage, and security posture all meet the minimum required thresholds. There is no override path.
phaseDescriptions.phase-8d-defect-management: The defect-manager triages, prioritizes, and tracks defects and missing implementations found during the release guard or completeness audit. This phase only runs when defects are detected or when required items are missing — otherwise it is skipped entirely.
phaseDescriptions.phase-9-deploy: Deployment strategy defines environment promotion rules, rollback criteria, and feature flags. A human approval gate provides the final sign-off before any environment is touched.
phaseDescriptions.phase-10-docs: Documentation-generator and doc-maintainer run asynchronously to sync all docs with the current system state. This phase never blocks the pipeline — documentation catches up in the background.
phaseDescriptions.phase-10b-export: The work-item-exporter exports the complete task graph to external project management systems as structured work items. This phase runs asynchronously alongside documentation and never blocks the pipeline.

stateScopes.label: Work Items
stateScopes.subtitle: Live task, bug, and CR item states

agentDiagram.sectionSubtitle: Agents are thin executors — all the intelligence lives in their skills.

agentDescriptions.analyzer: Extracts and structures requirements from raw input.
agentDescriptions.architect: Designs module boundaries, data flow, and API contracts.
agentDescriptions.planner: Breaks architecture into a task graph with estimates and milestones.
agentDescriptions.reviewer: Reviews code quality, SOLID principles, and security boundaries.
agentDescriptions.tester: Defines test strategies, coverage targets, and edge cases.
agentDescriptions.builder: Generates implementation code, repairs failures, and creates design systems.
agentDescriptions.impact-analyzer: Calculates the blast radius before any change is made.
agentDescriptions.test-generator: Generates unit, integration, and edge-case test suites from code.
agentDescriptions.deployer: Defines environment promotion rules, rollback criteria, and feature flags.
agentDescriptions.documenter: Generates API docs, ADRs, READMEs, and onboarding guides.
agentDescriptions.doc-maintainer: Detects outdated docs after every system change and syncs them.
agentDescriptions.recovery: Reverts to a prior system snapshot when a critical failure occurs.

workflowDiagram.sectionSubtitle: Every stage from installation to user review, showing how data flows through the system and where human decision points appear.

headline: From Idea
to Production
in a Single Prompt
useCaseHero.subheadline: Walk through exactly what happens after you describe a feature inside ASE-OS — step by step, from the moment you press Enter to the moment tested, reviewed, and deployment-ready code lands in your hands.

highlights.label: Non-bypassable Gates
highlights.desc: Human approval at every key decision

cta.title: Ready to build with ASE-OS?
cta.subtitle: {totalSkills} skills, {totalPipelinePhases} pipeline phases, and a fully wired multi-agent layer — ready to run inside any agentic development environment.

steps.title: Clone & Run Setup
steps.description: The full ASE-OS skill system lives in this repository. Clone it, then run setup — it installs the aiw CLI, checks prerequisites, and creates your .env in one step.

steps.title: Set Your GitHub Token & Launch
steps.description: Add your GitHub token to .env (classic PAT, no expiration, scopes: repo + read:org), then point the workflow at your project.
steps.code:
# Edit .env and set GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...
# Get one at: github.com/settings/tokens

aiw start /path/to/your-project

steps.title: The Orchestrator Takes Over
steps.description: The primary agent reads opencode.json and loads all skill bindings automatically. All {totalAgents} agents — including the analyzer, architect, builder, and domain specialists — are initialized and ready.
steps.code:
# opencode.json is the system entry point
# All agents and skill bindings are declared there

steps.title: Start with Requirements
steps.description: Describe what you want to build. The orchestrator routes your input through the full pipeline: requirements → architecture → planning → impact analysis → code generation → review → deployment.
steps.code:
# Example: describe your feature
"Build a user authentication system
with JWT tokens and role-based access"

steps.title: Approve Human Review Gates
steps.description: At critical points (after requirements, after architecture, before deployment), the pipeline pauses for your review and approval. You stay in control of every major decision.
steps.code:
# Pipeline pauses at:
# - Requirements validated       (phase 1)
# - Architecture approved        (phase 2)
# - UX & database design approved (phase 2b)
# - Design system approved       (phase 2c)
# - Roadmap approved             (phase 4)
# - Security posture approved    (phase 7)
# - Defect triage (conditional)  (phase 8d)
# - Final deploy approval        (phase 9)

steps.title: Add New Skills
steps.description: To extend the system, add a new SKILL.md file, register it in the three registry files, and it becomes part of every future pipeline run. The website updates on next build.
steps.code:
# New skill path:
.opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md

# Register in:
skills/index.yaml
skills/registry.json
skills/graph/skill-graph.yaml

installation.sectionSubtitle: Four commands. The orchestrator, all {totalSkills} skills, the knowledge graph, and {totalAgents} agents are ready in under a minute.

steps.title: Clone & Run Setup
steps.desc: Clone the repo and run ./aiw setup — it installs the aiw CLI, checks prerequisites, installs dependencies, and creates your .env in one step. No manual configuration needed.

steps.title: Set Token & Launch on Your Project
steps.desc: Add your GitHub token to .env (classic PAT, no expiration), then point the workflow at any project on your machine — or install it permanently into a project with aiw init.
steps.code:
# Edit .env — set GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...
# Get one at: github.com/settings/tokens

aiw start /path/to/your-project

# Or install into a project permanently:
aiw init /path/to/your-project

steps.title: Agents Auto-Initialize
steps.desc: The primary orchestrator reads opencode.json and auto-binds all {totalAgents} specialized subagents. No manual wiring — each agent's skill scope is declared in the config.

steps.title: Graph Index Built Automatically
steps.desc: Graphify scans the project AST and builds a knowledge graph used as long-term memory — no embeddings, no vector DB required.
steps.code:
# Runs automatically on first open via plugin hook
# graphify-out/ is populated with:
#   graph.json         — full AST graph
#   GRAPH_REPORT.md    — architecture summary
#   wiki/index.md      — navigable docs
graphify update .

about.name: Albadry Esmat
about.title: Creator of ASE-OS
about.bio: I build AI systems that do real engineering work — not just assistants that answer questions. ASE-OS is my attempt to create a complete, skill-driven operating system for software development: one that designs, generates, tests, reviews, and deploys code autonomously, with full traceability and no documentation drift.

followCta.subtitle: Follow me for updates on ASE-OS, AI engineering patterns, and new features as they ship.
